Phase-68.NAME: User Dashboard Subscription Merge and Cabinet Compaction
Phase-68.purpose: Make the authenticated user dashboard the compact primary working surface by removing low-value folds, moving subscription/tariff/calendar controls onto /dashboard, and preserving VPN, MTProto, config, and billing actions on mobile.

scope.in: User dashboard layout, MTProto card copy, user shell logo styling, subscription card/tariff/calendar presentation, user-cabinet button microinteractions, /dashboard/subscription route compatibility, frontend smoke contracts, and MyGRACE synchronization.
scope.out: Deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime, MTProto runtime, payment provider credentials, YooKassa webhook semantics, admin panel redesign, and backend price/device-limit ownership beyond approved tariff display names/descriptions.

requirements.requirement-1: The user shell logo must be frameless like the public/auth screens while staying visible in desktop sidebar and mobile top bar.
requirements.requirement-2: The dashboard must remove phase57-command-center and phase62-secondary-fold surfaces from the main page without hiding primary actions.
requirements.requirement-3: The MTProto card must remove "Индивидуальный Telegram proxy готов к использованию." and "Личный proxy"; it must show "Ваш бесплатный постоянный Telegram MTProto прокси" with Telegram MTProto visually emphasized.
requirements.requirement-4: The subscription page content must be moved under the MTProto card on /dashboard; /dashboard/subscription must remain non-breaking through redirect, anchor, or equivalent compatibility handling.
requirements.requirement-5: Pending trial copy must say "Конфиг уже доступен. Таймер на 4 дня стартует после первого подключения." and must not show the extra warning badge "Trial начнется после первого VPN подключения".
requirements.requirement-6: Remove phase57-card-compact phase62-inline-details subscription included/features blocks and remove "Детали тарифа" detail folds from every tariff card.
requirements.requirement-7: Move "Тарифные планы" above the calendar and remove helper copy about three tariffs, 30 days, and backend plan_id.
requirements.requirement-8: Rename user-visible tariffs to KrotPN Self, KrotPN Family, and KrotPN Team while preserving slugs, prices, duration, device limits, sort order, and backend-authoritative checkout shape.
requirements.requirement-9: Replace tariff icons and descriptions: Self uses "Персональный тариф"; Family uses "Тариф для семьи"; Team uses "Тариф для команды".
requirements.requirement-10: The calendar must be renamed "Календарь подписки", remove "Активные даты", become miniature/compact, and show cross-month subscription ranges intuitively.
requirements.requirement-11: All user-cabinet buttons should receive consistent Phase-59-style animated hover/active/copy feedback while respecting reduced-motion and avoiding layout shifts.
requirements.requirement-12: The config page must remove config-diagnostics and data-phase57-raw-config surfaces while preserving QR, .conf download, copy, device create/rotate/revoke, and protected config access.

steps.step-1: Audit Dashboard, Subscription, Config, Layout, CSS, and current Phase-57/62 smoke markers before editing to map removed, moved, and preserved surfaces.
steps.step-2: Apply frameless user-shell logo styling for desktop and mobile without changing brand assets or public/auth logo behavior.
steps.step-3: Remove dashboard phase57-command-center and phase62-secondary-fold blocks, then preserve any still-needed primary actions through compact direct controls.
steps.step-4: Rewrite MTProto card heading/copy, emphasize Telegram MTProto, keep tg:// app link, https://t.me browser link, manual server/port/secret copy, owner-only redaction, and 30s refresh.
steps.step-5: Extract subscription status, tariff catalog, checkout action, and calendar rendering into reusable dashboard-owned blocks and place them under the MTProto card.
steps.step-6: Keep /dashboard/subscription as a compatibility route that redirects to /dashboard subscription block or renders the same compact block without duplicating UI ownership.
steps.step-7: Update pending-first-connection copy, remove the extra warning badge, remove included-copy details, and keep server-derived countdown fields.
steps.step-8: Rename tariff display names/descriptions/icons and remove all tariff detail folds while preserving canonical slugs, prices, durations, limits, popularity, and plan_id-only checkout.
steps.step-9: Build the compact subscription calendar with cross-month range clarity, miniature sizing, no horizontal scroll, and accessible state text.
steps.step-10: Normalize animated button treatments across dashboard/config/referrals/settings user cabinet controls with reduced-motion fallbacks.
steps.step-11: Remove config diagnostics and raw config fallback panels while preserving QR/download/copy/device management and Phase-48 .conf download safeguards.
steps.step-12: Update Phase-45/50/57/62/61 smoke contracts and run frontend build, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard during execution.

data-flow.flow-1: /dashboard loads subscription, plans, devices, VPN stats, and MTProto owner state through existing APIs, then renders MTProto first and subscription/tariffs/calendar directly below it.
data-flow.flow-2: User chooses a tariff on /dashboard -> frontend posts plan_id only -> backend M-068/M-004 remains authoritative for price, duration, currency, and device limit.
data-flow.flow-3: Legacy /dashboard/subscription navigation -> compatibility route -> same subscription block or anchored /dashboard section, avoiding duplicated state and stale copy.
data-flow.flow-4: /dashboard/config keeps QR, download, copy, and device controls while removing diagnostic/raw-config surfaces from the visible cabinet.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: New verified user opens /dashboard on a phone: sees MTProto proxy, then subscription/tariffs/calendar without the old command center or secondary diagnostics.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: User with pending trial sees config-ready text and understands that the 4-day timer starts after first connection, without duplicate warning badges.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: User follows an old /dashboard/subscription link and still reaches the subscription controls without a broken route or duplicated bulky page.

verification-design.gate-1: Frontend build and Phase-57/62 smokes must confirm removed dashboard/config/subscription surfaces do not reappear.
verification-design.gate-2: Phase-45 smoke must confirm pending-first-VPN copy, countdown fields, and compact subscription calendar remain correct.
verification-design.gate-3: Phase-50 smoke must confirm tariff slugs/prices/device limits stay unchanged while user-visible names/descriptions/icons change as approved.
verification-design.gate-4: Phase-46/53/57 smokes must confirm MTProto owner links, Russian labels, redaction, and bounded refresh are preserved.
verification-design.gate-5: Phase-61 responsive smoke or screenshot proof must confirm no horizontal scroll, no text/icon overlap, and touch-safe buttons on phones/tablets.
verification-design.gate-6: Protected diff guard must fail the phase if deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/backend runtime or MTProto/VPN data-plane surfaces change unexpectedly.

commands.command: git diff --check; protected backend/deploy/runtime diff guard

execution-evidence.local-2026-06-04: Implemented shared frontend/src/components/SubscriptionPanel.tsx, embedded it below MTProto on /dashboard, kept /dashboard/subscription as a compatibility wrapper, removed visible dashboard command center/secondary fold and config raw/diagnostic surfaces, made the user shell logo frameless, and updated smoke contracts for the new ownership boundary.
execution-evidence.verification-2026-06-04: PASS: user/admin builds, Phase-68 smoke, Phase-45/46/50/53/57/58/60/61/62/63/67 regression smokes, git diff --check, MyGRACE lint via Phase-60, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guards passed locally.

risk-assessment.risk-1: Moving /dashboard/subscription onto /dashboard can break old navigation or tests unless the route remains compatible.
risk-assessment.risk-2: Tariff display-name changes must not mutate stable slugs, prices, device limits, or backend-authoritative checkout metadata unexpectedly.
risk-assessment.risk-3: Removing raw config/diagnostics must not remove QR, .conf download, copy, device management, or first pending-trial config access.
risk-assessment.risk-4: Button animations can create mobile layout shifts or reduced-motion issues unless bounded by existing M-077 constraints.

Phase-68.acceptance: /dashboard becomes the compact main cabinet with frameless logo, cleaned MTProto card, embedded subscription/tariffs/calendar, removed bulky secondary surfaces, preserved config actions, and passing frontend/MyGRACE verification.
